title: Electrical Foundations Capstone
summary: The final scene combines safety, math, wiring, panels, conduit, blueprint reading, AC/DC reasoning, and troubleshooting into one integrated apprentice-level mission.
learningObjectives: Apply foundational electrical theory across an entire job scenario.
Make safe decisions before, during, and after installation and diagnosis work.
Demonstrate readiness to connect drawings, calculations, measurements, and corrective action.

environment.setting: Full-jobsite simulation with plan table, service panel, rough-in area, conduit path, energized loads, and diagnostic fault station
environment.focus: Integrated performance rather than isolated topic practice

interactables.label: Scope and drawing package
interactables.teaches: Defines the installation and service objectives the learner must complete.

interactables.label: PPE and LOTO checkpoint
interactables.teaches: Blocks progress until safe preparation is complete.

interactables.label: Wiring, conduit, and panel work area
interactables.teaches: Requires correct layout, routing, device placement, and protection choices.

interactables.label: Test and troubleshoot station
interactables.teaches: Requires meter-based verification, fault diagnosis, and final documentation.

tasks: Read the drawing package and prepare the work plan.
Complete the required safety setup before touching equipment.
Install the assigned wiring and conduit path, then terminate the panel correctly.
Energize, test, diagnose one injected fault, repair it, and document the completed job.
completion: Learner completes the project with no critical safety violations.
Learner produces a functioning installation with correct protection and labeling.
Learner verifies performance and closes the capstone with accurate documentation.
